title: dubautia struthioloides
intro: Dubautia struthioloides, also known as Naupaka, is a flowering plant native to the Hawaiian Islands, characterized by its unique white and yellow flowers. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of Naupaka include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from fever and pain. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, the plant is prepared as a salve or infused in oil for topical application, or made into a tea for internal use.
